meta_title: Roofing in Driftwood, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in Driftwood, FL. CertainTeed & West Lake systems built for HVHZ. Licensed, local, A+ BBB. Free estimates!
h1: Driftwood's Trusted Roofing Experts: Hurricane-Rated Systems Built for South Florida's Toughest Conditions
intro:
Nestled just 1.5 miles from the Atlantic coastline and sitting squarely within Broward County's High Velocity Hurricane Zone, Driftwood is one of South Florida's most beautifully positioned — and most weather-exposed — communities. For homeowners in the Driftwood Community, that proximity to Hollywood North Beach Park and the ocean breezes that come with it isn't just a lifestyle perk; it's a daily reminder that your roof is the single most important layer of protection between your family and the elements. With average home values hovering around $360,000, a properly installed, code-compliant roof isn't just a safety measure — it's a smart financial investment in one of Broward County's most sought-after zip codes.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we understand the unique roofing demands placed on Driftwood homes. Approximately half of the homes in this area were built before 1994, meaning they predate the sweeping building code reforms that followed Hurricane Andrew. Many of those roofs — whether original flat systems, aging asphalt shingles, or clay tile installations — are now long past their useful service life and dangerously underequipped for the 170 mph wind zone that defines this stretch of South Florida. When a tropical storm or hurricane makes landfall, roof failure is typically the first domino to fall, and in a coastal community like Driftwood, that risk is amplified year after year.

Whether you're dealing with storm damage from a past hurricane season, watching a slow leak worsen with every afternoon thunderstorm, or simply planning a proactive upgrade before the next major storm threatens the coast between Hollywood and Dania Beach, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is the team Driftwood homeowners trust. With 60+ years of combined experience, 100% in-house installation crews, and a deep familiarity with the permitting process through the City of Hollywood Building Division, we bring the expertise and accountability that middle-income homeowners in this community need and deserve. We're not a fly-by-night contractor chasing storm chasers — we're your neighbors, and we're here for the long haul.

section_why.h2: Why Driftwood Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Full Roof Replacements and Repairs
section_why.body:
Choosing the right roofing contractor in a High Velocity Hurricane Zone isn't a decision to take lightly. In Driftwood, where coastal exposure, aging housing stock, and strict HVHZ code requirements all converge, you need a team that doesn't just talk about quality — they prove it on every job site, with every shingle, tile, and fastener. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has earned its reputation as 'The Kings of Service' by doing the job right, the first time, every time — and our A+ Better Business Bureau rating and 500+ completed homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties back that up.

One of the most important things that sets us apart is our commitment to 100% in-house installation. Unlike many roofing companies that rely on rotating subcontractors of varying skill and accountability, every Premier Impact Windows & Roofing crew member is a direct employee trained specifically for South Florida's demanding building standards. That means when we pull a permit through the City of Hollywood Building Division for your Driftwood home, the same team that signed on to the job is the one completing it — no handoffs, no strangers on your roof, no shortcuts. Our project managers are on-site regularly, and our installation processes are designed to meet and exceed HVHZ requirements from day one.

Our 60+ years of combined team experience means we've seen every roof type common to Driftwood: low-slope modified bitumen systems on ranch-style homes near the Driftwood Community, traditional concrete tile roofs that need updated underlayment, and architectural shingle systems that must be installed with hurricane-rated nailing patterns to meet today's code. We tailor every project to the specific age, structure, and exposure of your home — not a one-size-fits-all template. We also handle the full permitting process so you never have to navigate the City of Hollywood Building Division on your own.

Finally, we believe that quality roofing should be accessible to every Driftwood homeowner, not just those who can pay out of pocket. That's why we offer flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down programs available for qualified homeowners. You can protect your home, your family, and your $360,000 investment without draining your savings account — and we'll help you understand every financing option available to make that happen.

section_history.h2: Driftwood's Storm Legacy: What Hurricane Andrew, Wilma, and Irma Taught Us About South Florida Roofing
section_history.body:
Driftwood and the surrounding Hollywood area have been shaped — and scarred — by more than a decade's worth of major hurricane impacts. Since 1990, this stretch of Broward County has experienced 10 named storms significant enough to leave a mark. The three that loom largest in the memory of longtime residents are Hurricane Andrew in 1992, Hurricane Wilma in 2005, and Hurricane Irma in 2017 — three very different storms that collectively revealed just how vulnerable an aging roof can be when it meets sustained tropical-force winds.

Hurricane Andrew, which made landfall just south of here in Miami-Dade, fundamentally changed the way Florida approached building codes. For homes in Driftwood that existed before Andrew's August 1992 landfall, the structural and roofing standards they were built to were simply not sufficient for the storm that hit. The catastrophic damage triggered a complete overhaul of the Florida Building Code and gave birth to the HVHZ standards that govern coastal Broward County today. Hurricane Wilma, which cut across South Florida in October 2005, was a different kind of devastating — its rapid intensification and fast forward speed caught many homeowners off guard, tearing off roofing tiles and exposing underlayment systems that were never designed for that level of sustained wind. Hollywood and Dania Beach reported widespread roof damage, and Driftwood was no exception.

Hurricane Irma in 2017 served as a more recent wake-up call. Even as a weakened system by the time it reached Broward County, Irma exposed every crack in aging roofing systems — particularly on homes built in the 1970s and 1980s that make up roughly half of Driftwood's housing stock. The pattern is consistent: older roofs without proper hurricane-rated underlayment, inadequate fastening, or deteriorated flashing systems fail first. A modern, HVHZ-compliant roof from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is your most effective defense against the next storm season.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code and HVHZ Requirements Every Driftwood Homeowner Must Know
section_code.body:
Driftwood falls within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone, a designation that carries the strictest residential roofing standards in the entire United States. The HVHZ classification, codified under the Florida Building Code (FBC), applies to Miami-Dade and Broward counties and mandates that all roofing products and installation methods meet rigorous testing and approval standards before they can legally be used on any structure. This isn't optional guidance — it's the law, and it exists because the wind loads in this coastal corridor are genuinely extreme. Driftwood's 170 mph design wind speed means every roofing component must be engineered and installed to withstand forces that would devastate systems used elsewhere in the country.

For homeowners in the 33020 ZIP code, all roofing permits must be filed through the City of Hollywood Building Division, which enforces the Florida Building Code with full HVHZ provisions. This means your roofing contractor must use only HVHZ-approved products — each of which carries either a Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or a Florida Product Approval number. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we exclusively work with products that carry these designations, including our CertainTeed Landmark series shingles and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems, both of which are tested and approved for HVHZ use. Attempting to install non-approved products is not only a code violation — it can void your homeowner's insurance and result in a failed inspection that delays your project significantly.

Beyond product approval, HVHZ installation standards govern nailing patterns, underlayment specifications, and flashing requirements in ways that differ meaningfully from standard Florida roofing. For example, architectural shingles in the HVHZ must be installed with a specific nail count and placement to achieve their rated wind resistance — a detail that separates experienced HVHZ contractors from those who learned their trade in other parts of the state. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's crews are fully trained in HVHZ installation protocols, and we handle all inspections and permit close-outs through the City of Hollywood Building Division, so your project is fully code-compliant from start to finish.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Landmark Series Shingles and West Lake Tile: The Right Roofing Systems for Driftwood Homes
section_products.body:
When it comes to selecting the right roofing system for a coastal HVHZ community like Driftwood, not every product on the market makes the cut. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has carefully curated its product lineup to offer only systems that combine proven hurricane performance, long-term durability, and aesthetic appeal suited to the architectural character of South Florida homes. Our two flagship roofing lines — CertainTeed architectural shingles in the Landmark series and West Lake concrete and clay tile — represent the gold standard for residential roofing in Broward County.

The CertainTeed Landmark series is one of the most recognized and respected architectural shingle lines in the country, and for good reason. These shingles feature a dual-layer construction that delivers exceptional dimensional depth and visual richness while providing the structural integrity needed for HVHZ environments. Landmark series shingles carry Miami-Dade and Florida Product Approvals, and when installed with the proper HVHZ nailing pattern, they provide outstanding wind resistance that protects Driftwood homes through the most active storm seasons. They're also available in a wide range of colors and profiles, making it easy to refresh your home's curb appeal while simultaneously fortifying its structural envelope. For homeowners in the Driftwood Community who want a high-performing roof that complements their home's value without the premium price point of tile, CertainTeed Landmark is an outstanding choice.

For homeowners drawn to the classic Mediterranean and Spanish Colonial aesthetics common throughout Broward County, our West Lake concrete and clay tile systems offer unparalleled longevity and storm performance. West Lake tile is specifically engineered for Florida's climate — resisting moisture, UV degradation, salt air exposure, and high-wind events that would compromise lesser tile systems. Clay tile, in particular, has been used in coastal South Florida for generations precisely because of its durability; properly installed West Lake clay tile can last 50 years or more with minimal maintenance. Both West Lake concrete and clay tile options carry HVHZ approvals and are installed by our experienced in-house crews using the correct mortar, fastening, and underlayment systems required by the Florida Building Code.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in Driftwood, FL? Honest Pricing and Flexible Financing Options
section_cost.body:
For Driftwood homeowners with properties valued around $360,000, a full roof replacement is one of the most consequential investments you can make — and one of the most financially sound. Roofing costs in Broward County's HVHZ vary depending on roof size, pitch, material selection, and the extent of any underlying deck damage, but as a general benchmark, homeowners can expect full replacement costs to range from $12,000 to $25,000 or more for architectural shingle systems, with tile roofs typically running higher depending on square footage and tile type. These numbers reflect HVHZ-compliant installation with proper permitting through the City of Hollywood Building Division — not the lowball figures you might see from unlicensed contractors who skip code requirements and cut corners on materials.

The return on that investment is substantial and multi-layered. A new HVHZ-compliant roof directly protects your home's structural integrity and interior contents during hurricane season — a category of protection that is quite literally priceless when a major storm makes landfall. But the financial returns are also very real: insurance premium reductions, increased resale value, and elimination of ongoing repair costs that drain middle-income homeowners month after month. Realtors across the Hollywood and Dania Beach markets consistently report that a newer roof is one of the top value-adding improvements a homeowner can make before listing, often returning 60-70% or more of the project cost at resale.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes these upgrades accessible with flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month, with $0 down programs available for qualified applicants. We work with multiple lending partners to find the right solution for your budget, and our team will walk you through every option during your free estimate appointment. You shouldn't have to choose between financial comfort and the safety of your home — and with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's financing programs, you don't have to.

section_insurance.h2: Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance Premiums with a Hurricane-Rated Roof in Driftwood
section_insurance.body:
One of the most meaningful — and often underappreciated — financial benefits of replacing your roof in Driftwood is the impact it can have on your homeowner's insurance premiums. Florida's property insurance market is notoriously volatile, and Broward County homeowners are among those paying some of the highest premiums in the state due to coastal exposure and the HVHZ designation. However, a new hurricane-rated roof opens the door to significant credits through the Florida Wind Mitigation Credit program, which requires insurers to apply discounts when a home meets specific construction standards.

The wind mitigation inspection process, conducted by a licensed inspector after your new roof is completed, evaluates features like roof shape, roof deck attachment, roof-to-wall connection, and — most critically — the opening protection and roof covering itself. A new HVHZ-compliant roof with proper nail spacing, approved underlayment, and a Miami-Dade or Florida Product Approved covering can dramatically improve your wind mitigation report scores. Depending on your current insurer and policy, Driftwood homeowners have reported premium reductions ranging from 15% to 40% after a qualifying roof replacement. On a policy that costs $4,000 to $6,000 annually — not unusual for a coastal Broward County home — that translates to hundreds or even thousands of dollars in annual savings.

Many major carriers operating in South Florida, including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Insurance, actively incentivize wind mitigation upgrades through their rating structures. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing will provide you with all documentation needed to submit a wind mitigation report to your insurer after project completion, and we can advise you on what features of your new roof will carry the most weight in your specific policy's rating calculation. The combination of reduced premiums, increased home value, and storm protection makes a Premier Impact Windows & Roofing roof one of the smartest financial decisions a Driftwood homeowner can make.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Driftwood home needs a full roof replacement or just a repair?
faqs.a: The answer depends on the age of your roof, the extent of damage, and whether your existing system meets current HVHZ standards. Homes in Driftwood built before 1994 are especially likely to have roofing systems that fall short of today's Florida Building Code requirements, even if they appear structurally intact. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free on-site assessments where our experienced team will evaluate your roof's condition, identify any hidden damage from storms like Irma or Wilma, and give you an honest recommendation — repair when appropriate, replacement when necessary.

faqs.q: How much does a roof replacement cost in Driftwood, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Full roof replacements in Driftwood typically range from $12,000 to $25,000 or more, depending on roof size, pitch, material type, and any underlying deck damage discovered during tear-off. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. We work with multiple lending partners to ensure Driftwood homeowners can access the protection they need without financial strain. Contact us for a free estimate and personalized financing consultation.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my roof in Driftwood, FL, and how does the HVHZ affect the process?
faqs.a: Yes — a permit is required for all roof replacements in Driftwood, and permits are filed through the City of Hollywood Building Division. Because Driftwood is located in the High Velocity Hurricane Zone, all materials must carry Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance or Florida Product Approval, and installation must follow HVHZ-specific protocols for nailing patterns, underlayment, and flashing. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf, from application through final inspection, ensuring full compliance with all applicable Florida Building Code provisions.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take for a typical home in the Driftwood Community?
faqs.a: For most single-family homes in the Driftwood Community, a full roof replacement takes between one and three days for the physical installation, assuming no significant deck damage is discovered. Permit processing through the City of Hollywood Building Division can add one to three weeks to the overall project timeline depending on current workload. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing coordinates all scheduling, inspections, and material delivery to minimize disruption to your household. We'll give you a clear timeline during your estimate appointment and keep you updated throughout the project.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowner's insurance in Driftwood?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. A new HVHZ-compliant roof in Driftwood qualifies your home for Florida's Wind Mitigation Credit program, which mandates that insurers apply discounts for homes meeting specific construction standards. After installation, a licensed wind mitigation inspector will document your roof's new features, and that report is submitted to your insurance carrier. Driftwood homeowners with coastal exposure and older homes have reported premium reductions of 15% to 40% following qualifying roof replacements. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all documentation needed to support your wind mitigation claim.
